title: Customer missing mobile app and buyer landing page on agent-teams plan
description:
New support request from: @koala.cx

> **Request Type:**
> Bug | Troubleshoot Issue
> **Relevant Area:**
> Customer - Power-Ups
> **Explanation of Request**
> Customer Alex Alpha and her team are on an agent-teams plan but the mobile app and buyers landing page features aren't showing up. She's been on this plan for 2 weeks and these should be included. Can we get this looked at?
> **Customer First and Last Name**
> Alex Alpha
> **Customer HB Admin or Email Address**
> https://hbadmin.homebot.rocks/employee/users/abc12345-fake-uuid
rationale: Individual customer on agent-teams plan missing features. Likely a plan config issue (full_access toggle). VSB owns individual customer account setup.

title: HBN partner showing as deactivated in co-sponsor search
description:
New support request from: @dolphin.csm

> **Request Type:**
> Bug | Troubleshoot Issue
> **Relevant Area:**
> Other (Please specify below)
> **Explanation of Request**
> Trying to add LO Bailey Bravo as a co-sponsor to REA Carmen Charlie's account. Bailey has an active Unlimited subscription but when I search for him in HB Admin, he shows up with a "preferred partner" tag and the system won't let me add him as a co-sponsor. His HB Admin page shows active status.
> **Customer First and Last Name**
> Bailey Bravo
> **Customer HB Admin or Email Address**
> https://hbadmin.homebot.rocks/employee/users/def67890-fake-uuid
rationale: Co-sponsorship management in HB Admin is owned by Customer Experience. The preferred partner tag mismatch is a CEP/HBN data state issue.

title: Welcome emails not sending for newly uploaded clients
description:
New support request from: @penguin.am

> **Request Type:**
> General Question
> **Relevant Area:**
> Email Deliverability
> **Explanation of Request**
> LO Drew Delta uploaded about 200 clients last week and turned on welcome emails yesterday. He's now sending his first manual digest but wants to know — will those 200 clients get a welcome email with their first digest, or did he miss the window since they were uploaded before the setting was on?
> **Customer First and Last Name**
> Drew Delta
> **Customer HB Admin or Email Address**
> https://hbadmin.homebot.rocks/employee/users/ghi11111-fake-uuid
rationale: This is a question about platform behavior, not a bug. The answer is in the codebase (client.rb welcome email eligibility checks). If no digests have been sent yet, the clients will get welcome emails. If digests already went out, they won't.

title: Enterprise customer requesting bulk migration of 5,000 clients to new LO assignments
description:
New support request from: @panda.sales

> **Request Type:**
> Account Management
> **Relevant Area:**
> Enterprise Subscription
> **Explanation of Request**
> Acme Lending Co (enterprise customer) is restructuring their LO territories. They need approximately 5,000 clients reassigned from 12 departing LOs to 8 new LOs. They've provided a CSV mapping file. This needs to happen before their next digest send on the 15th.
> **Link to enterprise subscription**
> https://hbadmin.homebot.rocks/enterprise/acme-lending-co
> **Customer First and Last Name**
> Riley Romeo (Acme Lending Co admin)
rationale: Bulk migration for an enterprise customer. Enterprise team handles bulk changes and enterprise-specific operations.

title: Home values showing significantly off for clients in Austin, TX area
description:
New support request from: @koala.cx

> **Request Type:**
> Bug | Troubleshoot Issue
> **Relevant Area:**
> Client - Home Value
> **Explanation of Request**
> REA Elliot Echo reports that several of her clients in the Austin, TX 78704 zip code are seeing home values that are way off — some showing $150K when they should be $500K+. She's gotten complaints from 6 different clients this week. The addresses look correct in HB Admin but the valuations seem wrong.
> **Customer First and Last Name**
> Elliot Echo
> **Customer HB Admin or Email Address**
> https://hbadmin.homebot.rocks/employee/users/jkl22222-fake-uuid
rationale: Home value data accuracy issue for a specific geographic area. This is underlying data quality, not an app display bug. Data team owns home valuations and address matching.

title: Digest emails not being delivered for any customers since this morning
description:
New support request from: @fox.mktg

> **Request Type:**
> Bug | Troubleshoot Issue
> **Relevant Area:**
> Email Deliverability
> **Explanation of Request**
> Multiple customers are reporting that their scheduled digest emails did not go out this morning. We've gotten 15+ reports in the last 2 hours from different customers across different plans. Manual sends also appear to be failing. This seems like a widespread issue, not customer-specific.
> **Customer First and Last Name**
> N/A (multiple)
> **Customer HB Admin or Email Address**
> N/A
rationale: Widespread email delivery failure across multiple customers. This is send infrastructure, not content. User Messaging owns the email delivery pipeline and channels.

title: Client home digest showing wrong listing photos — photos belong to different properties
description:
New support request from: @dolphin.csm

> **Request Type:**
> Bug | Troubleshoot Issue
> **Relevant Area:**
> Client - Home Digest
> **Explanation of Request**
> A client of LO Finley Foxtrot received their home digest and the listing photos in the "Listings for You" section are mismatched — the photos shown don't correspond to the actual listings. The addresses and prices are correct but the images are from completely different properties. Client sent a screenshot showing this.
> **Customer First and Last Name**
> Finley Foxtrot
> **Customer HB Admin or Email Address**
> https://hbadmin.homebot.rocks/employee/users/mno33333-fake-uuid
rationale: Listing photo data comes from the upstream data pipeline (thumbnail_image column). The application passes this field through without transformation — if addresses and prices are correct but photos are wrong, the corruption is in the data ingestion layer, not the app.
edge_case_note: Surfaces as a Client Experience issue (wrong photos in digest) but both eval agents independently traced the data pipeline and proved the app just passes thumbnail_image through. Root cause is upstream data quality.

title: Customer's welcome email content showing garbled characters instead of spaces
description:
New support request from: @penguin.am

> **Request Type:**
> Bug | Troubleshoot Issue
> **Relevant Area:**
> Customer - Email Customization
> **Explanation of Request**
> REA Gray Golf customized her welcome email content in the CEP editor, but when clients receive the email, all spaces are replaced with ? characters. The preview in the editor looks fine but the sent email is garbled. She pasted the text from a Word document.
> **Customer First and Last Name**
> Gray Golf
> **Customer HB Admin or Email Address**
> https://hbadmin.homebot.rocks/employee/users/pqr44444-fake-uuid
rationale: Email content/encoding issue originating from the CEP email editor. This is about the content, not the delivery infrastructure. The encoding bug is in the editor or content processing pipeline, which CX owns. NOT User Messaging — they own send channels, not content.
edge_case_note: The 'Email' label and 'Email Deliverability' area might pull toward User Messaging, but the issue is content encoding from the CEP editor, not send infrastructure. CX owns the editor.

title: Request to add AI-powered market summary to home digest
description:
New support request from: @panda.sales

> **Request Type:**
> Feature Request
> **Relevant Area:**
> Client - Home Digest
> **Explanation of Request**
> Enterprise customer Acme Realty Group has requested that we add an AI-generated local market summary section to the home digest. They think it would help their agents provide more value to clients. They're willing to beta test it. Can we get this in front of product?
> **Customer First and Last Name**
> Acme Realty Group (enterprise)
> **Customer HB Admin or Email Address**
> N/A
rationale: This is a feature request, not a bug or support issue. Should be closed with a note that this is a feature request and the appropriate channel for product feedback.

title: Loan data showing incorrect interest rates for FHA loans in client reports
description:
New support request from: @koala.cx

> **Request Type:**
> Bug | Troubleshoot Issue
> **Relevant Area:**
> Client - Home Value
> **Explanation of Request**
> Several LOs have reported that the FHA loan rates shown in their clients' home digests are significantly off from current market rates. The rates appear to be about 2 months stale. This is affecting how the refinance savings calculations look to clients and making the LOs look bad.
> **Customer First and Last Name**
> Multiple LOs reporting
> **Customer HB Admin or Email Address**
> N/A
rationale: Stale loan rate data is a data quality/pipeline issue. The rates feed into the home digest but the root cause is the data being out of date, not a display bug. Data team owns loan data and rate feeds.
edge_case_note: Surfaces as a client experience issue (wrong info in digest) but the through-line is stale data in the loan rate pipeline. Data team, not Client Experience.

title: Recurly showing duplicate subscriptions after HBN partner upgraded to paid plan
description:
New support request from: @fox.mktg

> **Request Type:**
> Bug | Troubleshoot Issue
> **Relevant Area:**
> Customer - Billing
> **Explanation of Request**
> After REA Harper Hotel upgraded from HBN Preferred Partner to a paid Agent Partner plan, Recurly shows two active subscriptions — the old HBN preferred partner plan with a far-future expiration and the new paid plan. The customer is concerned about being double-billed. This is the third time we've seen this in the past month.
> **Customer First and Last Name**
> Harper Hotel
> **Customer HB Admin or Email Address**
> https://hbadmin.homebot.rocks/employee/users/stu55555-fake-uuid
rationale: HBN upgrade flow issue. While it manifests as a billing problem in Recurly, the root cause is the HBN-to-paid upgrade path not properly closing out the old subscription. CX owns HBN and the upgrade flow. Should flag the Recurly/billing angle in the comment.
edge_case_note: Could be VSB since it touches billing/subscription for an individual customer. But the upgrade flow from HBN preferred partner to paid is an HBN feature path, which CX owns.

title: Partner intel data showing wrong brokerage for LO in public records
description:
New support request from: @dolphin.csm

> **Request Type:**
> Bug | Troubleshoot Issue
> **Relevant Area:**
> Other (Please specify below)
> **Explanation of Request**
> LO Indigo India's partner intel page is showing him as affiliated with "Acme Bank Alpha" but he moved to "Acme Bank Bravo" 6 months ago. His NMLS record is correct on the NMLS site but our data hasn't updated. This is showing up in his HBN profile and when other agents see his partner intel info.
> **Customer First and Last Name**
> Indigo India
> **Customer HB Admin or Email Address**
> https://hbadmin.homebot.rocks/employee/users/vwx66666-fake-uuid
rationale: Partner intel data sourced from public records (NMLS) is stale. This is a data pipeline/ingestion issue — the public record data pull isn't reflecting updated NMLS records. Data team owns public record data feeds.

title: Homebot website completely down — 503 errors across all pages
description:
New support request from: @fox.mktg

> **Request Type:**

> Other (Please specify below)

> Multiple reports that homebot.ai is returning 503 errors. Customer admin, client pages, and the marketing site are all affected. Started about 20 minutes ago. Getting flooded with support tickets about this.
> **Customer First and Last Name**
> N/A (site-wide)
> **Customer HB Admin or Email Address**
> N/A
rationale: Site-wide outage with 503 errors across all surfaces. Infrastructure owns site availability and serving.

title: Customer CEP showing wrong client count — dashboard says 500 but HB Admin shows 1,200
description:
New support request from: @penguin.am

> **Request Type:**
> Bug | Troubleshoot Issue
> **Relevant Area:**
> Customer - Dashboard
> **Explanation of Request**
> LO Jordan Juliet sees 500 clients on her CEP dashboard but her HB Admin profile shows 1,200 active clients. She recently had a bulk upload of about 700 clients done by her enterprise admin. The new clients appear in HB Admin but not in her CEP client list or dashboard count.
> **Customer First and Last Name**
> Jordan Juliet
> **Customer HB Admin or Email Address**
> https://hbadmin.homebot.rocks/employee/users/yza77777-fake-uuid
rationale: CEP dashboard showing incorrect client count. The clients exist in the backend (HB Admin confirms them) but the CEP dashboard/client list isn't reflecting them. This is a CX display or sync issue in the customer-facing application.
edge_case_note: Could be a data sync issue if the bulk upload pipeline didn't properly associate clients in all systems. But since the clients exist in HB Admin, the data is there — it's the CEP not displaying it. CX first, with a note about potential data sync angle.

title: Mobile app crashing on launch for Android clients after latest update
description:
New support request from: @panda.sales

> **Request Type:**
> Bug | Troubleshoot Issue
> **Relevant Area:**
> Client - Mobile App
> **Explanation of Request**
> Getting multiple reports from LOs that their clients' Android Homebot app is crashing immediately on launch since the latest app update (v4.2.1). Seems to affect Android 13+ devices specifically. At least 10 different clients reported across 4 different LOs.
> **Customer First and Last Name**
> Multiple clients
> **Customer HB Admin or Email Address**
> N/A
rationale: Mobile app crash affecting clients (consumers). Client Experience owns the consumer-facing mobile app.

title: Client not receiving any emails — digest, welcome, or notifications
description:
New support request from: @panda.sales

> **Request Type:**
> Bug | Troubleshoot Issue
> **Relevant Area:**
> Email Deliverability
> **Explanation of Request**
> Client Kai Kilo (homeowner) says he hasn't received any emails from Homebot in 3 months — no digests, no welcome email, nothing. His LO Logan Lima confirms he's in her client list and digests are enabled. She's tried manually sending to him and it doesn't arrive. Other clients of hers receive emails fine. Kai checked spam and it's not there.
> **Customer First and Last Name**
> Logan Lima (LO) / Kai Kilo (client)
> **Customer HB Admin or Email Address**
> https://hbadmin.homebot.rocks/employee/users/amb11111-fake-uuid
rationale: Could be User Messaging (email delivery/bounce issue for this specific address), Client Experience (client-side email suppression or unsubscribe), or Data (client record issue). The right team depends on investigation — is the email bouncing? Is the client unsubscribed? Is there a suppression list entry? Should flag uncertainty and investigate before routing.
edge_case_note: Genuinely ambiguous. Single client not receiving emails could be delivery infrastructure (User Messaging), client record state (CLE), or even a data issue. Investigation needed to narrow it down.

title: Customer's clients seeing different home values in mobile app vs email digest
description:
New support request from: @dolphin.csm

> **Request Type:**
> Bug | Troubleshoot Issue
> **Relevant Area:**
> Client - Home Value
> **Explanation of Request**
> LO Morgan Mike reports that his client Nico November sees a home value of $425K in her email digest but $390K in the mobile app for the same property. Both were received/viewed on the same day. Which one is correct? This is confusing his client.
> **Customer First and Last Name**
> Morgan Mike
> **Customer HB Admin or Email Address**
> https://hbadmin.homebot.rocks/employee/users/amb22222-fake-uuid
rationale: Home value discrepancy between two client surfaces (email digest vs mobile app). Could be Client Experience (different rendering/calculation in two surfaces), Data (two surfaces pulling from different data sources or cached at different times), or even native-backend (mobile app pulling from a different API). Needs investigation to determine if it's a display issue, a caching issue, or a data source mismatch.
edge_case_note: Touches multiple teams. The email digest is CLE, the mobile app is CLE, but the underlying home value could be Data. The discrepancy between two surfaces suggests either a caching/timing issue or two different data paths.

title: New customer signed up but HB Admin shows no subscription and Recurly has no record
description:
New support request from: @penguin.am

> **Request Type:**
> Bug | Troubleshoot Issue
> **Relevant Area:**
> Customer - Billing
> **Explanation of Request**
> REA Oakley Oscar signed up for an Agent Solo plan through the website 3 days ago. He has a Lockbox account and can log in, but HB Admin shows no active subscription and Recurly has no record of him. He says he was charged on his credit card. He's stuck in a limited state and can't access paid features.
> **Customer First and Last Name**
> Oakley Oscar
> **Customer HB Admin or Email Address**
> https://hbadmin.homebot.rocks/employee/users/amb33333-fake-uuid
rationale: Signup completed (Lockbox account exists, card was charged) but subscription never created in Recurly/Mikasa. Could be VSB (individual customer account setup), Customer Experience (onboarding flow bug), or Infrastructure (webhook failure between payment processor and Recurly/Mikasa). The signup flow spans multiple systems.
edge_case_note: Spans the boundary between VSB (account setup), CX (onboarding flow), and potentially Infrastructure (webhook/integration failure). The charge happened but the subscription didn't propagate.

title: LO's partner intel volume number doesn't match what she expects
description:
New support request from: @dolphin.csm

> **Request Type:**
> Bug | Troubleshoot Issue
> **Relevant Area:**
> Partner Intel
> **Explanation of Request**
> One of our users, LO Payton Papa, noticed her volume in Homebot's partner intel page is not accurate. Her admin Quinn Quebec reached out to inquire. Payton says the number is lower than what she's actually produced this year. Could someone take a look, and also let me know where the numbers are pulling from?
> **Customer First and Last Name**
> Payton Papa (Quinn Quebec, admin, reached out to inquire)
> **Customer HB Admin or Email Address**
> https://hbadmin.homebot.rocks/employee/users/int19000-fake-uuid
rationale: Partner Intel data accuracy questions should go to Customer Experience for initial investigation, not Intelligence or Data. First-pass code review should check the volume calculation path (customer-admin → mikasa markethub → fact_loan SUM). If the app layer is clean, the root cause is usually upstream attribution (NMLS ID missing in fact_loan) or product criteria for what counts as an LO's volume. If investigation shifts to product criteria questions, tag Chris Johnson on the Linear ticket.
edge_case_note: Old behavior routed this to Intelligence (which held the original INT-284) or Data (public records angle). New shortcut in the skill routes it to CX first for a code/data check, with Chris Johnson as product escalation for criteria questions. Tests the Partner Intel known-shortcut routing.

title: Follow up on unresolved escalation — CEP dashboard client count still off weeks later
description:
New support request from: @dolphin.csm

> **Request Type:**
> Bug | Troubleshoot Issue
> **Relevant Area:**
> Customer - Dashboard
> **Explanation of Request**
> Resurfacing an escalation from earlier this quarter that still hasn't been resolved. LO reached out today looking for an update. Linking the original ticket to this one. From the last update on that ticket, the engineer was looking to confirm specific info from the customer for further investigation. Can we get confirmation so we can proceed toward a resolution? Customer is getting frustrated.
> **Original Ticket**
> https://linear.app/homebot/issue/CUX-100/cep-client-count-discrepancy
> **Customer First and Last Name**
> Jordan Juliet
> **Customer HB Admin or Email Address**
> https://hbadmin.homebot.rocks/employee/users/amb20000-fake-uuid
rationale: Follow-up ticket resurfacing a stalled escalation. The routing comment should note this is a follow-up on the linked original ticket and route to the same team that owns the original (Customer Experience, based on CEP dashboard ownership). Don't re-triage from scratch — keep it with the team that already has context on the underlying investigation.
edge_case_note: Follow-up pattern: the ticket surfaces a previously-dispatched issue that stalled. The routing should point back to where the original lives and note that the follow-up is asking for movement, not a fresh investigation.

title: Subject line shows 'Y' in home search email to prospects
description:
New support request from: @otter.supp

> **Request Type:**
> Bug | Troubleshoot Issue
> **Relevant Area:**
> Email Deliverability
> **Explanation of Request**
> The subject line of our home search email is showing just the letter "Y" when sent to prospects. Screenshot attached. This is going to buyer/prospect clients of REA Reese Sierra.
> **Customer First and Last Name**
> Reese Sierra
> **Customer HB Admin or Email Address**
> https://hbadmin.homebot.rocks/employee/users/amb21000-fake-uuid
rationale: Prospect-facing email content bug — the subject line is wrong. Email content issues go to the team that owns the content (prospect/client emails), not User Messaging (which owns delivery infrastructure). Client Experience owns consumer-facing home search reports. A first-pass code review of the prospect email subject generation (home_search_report dynamic_data, prospect_single_listing, saved_search) should be included in the routing comment to give CLE a head start.
edge_case_note: The 'Email Deliverability' label might pull toward User Messaging, but the issue is subject line content, not delivery. CLE owns prospect-facing emails. The agent should investigate the subject-generation code before routing — all paths use i18n translations that produce proper strings, so the bug likely lives in production data or SendGrid template rendering, which is worth noting in the comment.

title: Multiple CEP maintenance list bugs — filtering, counts, and empty lists
description:
New support request from: @dolphin.csm

> **Request Type:**
> Bug | Troubleshoot Issue
> **Relevant Area:**
> Customer - CEP Issue or Question
> **Explanation of Request**
> Customer has three related issues on the Maintenance lists section of CEP.
>
> 1. When filtering clients by Maintenance > Email Issues > Spam Complaints, only clients with the Invalid Email tag surface. Clicking in shows they have both Invalid Email and Spam Complaint tags, but the list only displays the Invalid Email tag.
>
> 2. The total count in the list header stays at "x clients with email issues" even after filtering down by a specific issue type. Also, the previous UI let him expand page size up to 100 results per page; that option is gone.
>
> 3. Dashboard > Highly Engaged Clients > See all shows "No clients currently on this list" despite the Dashboard card showing there are clients in that list.
> **Customer First and Last Name**
> Sage Tango
> **Customer HB Admin or Email Address**
> https://hbadmin.homebot.rocks/employee/users/amb22000-fake-uuid
rationale: Three CEP client dashboard bugs bundled in one ticket. All three live in the CEP surface that CX owns: maintenance list filtering/tag display, list pagination and count logic, and the Highly Engaged Clients list rendering. The ticket should go to CX with all three bugs listed in the routing comment so the team sees the full scope.

title: AUTO-MODE TEST — LO volume dispute (simulating missing repos)
description:
New support request from: @dolphin.csm

> **Request Type:**
> Bug | Troubleshoot Issue
> **Relevant Area:**
> Partner Intel
> **Explanation of Request**
> LO Taylor Uniform says his partner intel volume is showing way less than what he's actually produced over the past year. He shared screenshots comparing the Homebot number with his internal CRM number. Customer wants this resolved quickly — they're threatening to churn over data credibility.
> **Customer First and Last Name**
> Taylor Uniform
> **Customer HB Admin or Email Address**
> https://hbadmin.homebot.rocks/employee/users/amb23000-fake-uuid
rationale: This is the same class of ticket as T19 (Partner Intel accuracy question) and should route to Customer Experience per the Known Shortcut. The eval variant tests auto-mode behavior when local repos are unavailable. Success criteria: (1) routes to CX without stalling or pausing; (2) the routing comment contains zero user-facing commentary about missing repos, freshness nudges, or any meta-prompts; (3) the agent proceeds in routing-only mode silently as specified in the skill's Prerequisites section; (4) the routing comment is actionable for CX even without code-trace findings.
edge_case_note: Tests auto-mode silent degradation when ~/code/homebot is unavailable. The skill should route on ticket content and the routing guide alone. Any text in the result.md mentioning missing repos, suggesting clones, or nudging the user to pull is a FAIL — those belong only in non-auto mode.

title: AUTO-MODE TEST — CEP dashboard bug (simulating partial repos + auto mode)
description:
New support request from: @penguin.am

> **Request Type:**
> Bug | Troubleshoot Issue
> **Relevant Area:**
> Customer - Dashboard
> **Explanation of Request**
> REA Umi Victor's CEP dashboard is showing duplicate entries in her client list — some clients appear twice with slightly different creation dates but the same name and email. She noticed it after a bulk import last week. Count in header shows 450, list shows 320 unique clients.
> **Customer First and Last Name**
> Umi Victor
> **Customer HB Admin or Email Address**
> https://hbadmin.homebot.rocks/employee/users/amb24000-fake-uuid
rationale: CEP dashboard duplicate-client display bug — CX owns the dashboard and the client list UI. The eval variant tests auto-mode behavior with partial repo coverage: customer-admin (the key repo) is NOT available, but other repos are. Success criteria: (1) routes confidently to CX without asking about missing repos; (2) no freshness nudge or clone suggestion appears anywhere in the output; (3) routing comment is still useful even without customer-admin code context; (4) no meta-commentary like 'would have benefited from customer-admin'.
edge_case_note: Tests auto-mode silent degradation with partial repos. The skill says in non-auto mode the agent may mention which missing repos would have helped, but in auto mode it must stay silent. A routing comment that quietly works around the missing repo is a PASS; any meta-text about missing repos is a FAIL.
